channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: French government seeking release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
item.description: The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.
item.content:encoded:
The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A Colorado hospital profits from resolving language barriers
item.description: Without qualified interpreters at doctors' offices, non-English speakers can face bad — even fatal — health outcomes. A hospital in rural Colorado is training its existing bilingual staff to address the service gap.
item.content:encoded:
Without qualified interpreters at doctors' offices, non-English speakers can face bad — even fatal — health outcomes. A hospital in rural Colorado is training its existing bilingual staff to address the service gap.

(Image credit: Ashlie Bramley

)
item.dc:creator: Halle Zander

item.title: What Georgia swing voters say about Trump, the Iran war and the cost of living
item.description: The war in Iran has been dragging down President Trump's approval rating. A new focus group of swing voters finds broad displeasure with the president's handling of that war.
item.content:encoded: The war in Iran has been dragging down President Trump's approval rating. A new focus group of swing voters finds broad displeasure with the president's handling of that war.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
item.description: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

(Image credit: Alberto Pizzoli)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
item.description: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: No one likes being discombobulated. How did the feeling get such a fun name?
item.description: Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.

(Image credit: Leo Patrizi)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 'No peace': Nearly a year after her son's death, she learned that ICE was responsible
item.description: Ruben Ray Martinez is considered the first person to be killed by ICE during President Trump's second term. His mother believes his death could have been avoided.
item.content:encoded:
Ruben Ray Martinez is considered the first person to be killed by ICE during President Trump's second term. His mother believes his death could have been avoided.

(Image credit: Brenda Bazán for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: A public radio station in Ohio needed a new home. Comedian Dave Chappelle stepped up
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with comedian Dave Chappelle about his investments in public media, the art of standup comedy and the town of Yellow Springs, Ohio.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with comedian Dave Chappelle about his investments in public media, the art of standup comedy and the town of Yellow Springs, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Georgia jail uses drones to block dangerous deliveries to inmates
item.description: The Fulton County jail in Georgia is using drones to intercept deliveries of dangerous contraband to inmates. The drones are made by Flock, a company under scrutiny for its surveillance reach.
item.content:encoded: The Fulton County jail in Georgia is using drones to intercept deliveries of dangerous contraband to inmates. The drones are made by Flock, a company under scrutiny for its surveillance reach.
item.dc:creator: Chamian Cruz

item.title: Middle East conflict causes another shortage: fluoride for drinking water
item.description: A shortage of chemicals used to fluoridate water, tied to the conflict in the Middle East, is causing some public water systems to reduce fluoride levels in public drinking water.
item.content:encoded: A shortage of chemicals used to fluoridate water, tied to the conflict in the Middle East, is causing some public water systems to reduce fluoride levels in public drinking water.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang
